content.problemStatement:
Uttu broke his phone. He can get it repaired by spending XXX rupees or he can buy a new phone by spending YYY rupees. Uttu wants to spend as little money as possible. Find out if it is better to get the phone repaired or to buy a new phone.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers XXX and YYY — the cost of getting the phone repaired and the cost of buying a new phone.


Output Format
For each test case,



output `REPAIR` if it is better to get the phone repaired.
output `NEW PHONE` if it is better to buy a new phone.
output `ANY` if both the options have the same price.

You may print each character of `REPAIR`, `NEW PHONE` and `ANY` in uppercase or lowercase (for example, `any`, `ANy`, `Any` will be considered identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y — the cost to repair the phone and the cost to buy a new phone.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case output on a separate line:
- "REPAIR" if X < Y
- "NEW PHONE" if X > Y
- "ANY" if X = Y

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 100 < 1000 → REPAIR.\nTest case 2: 10000 > 5000 → NEW PHONE.\nTest case 3: 3000 = 3000 → ANY.
Repair cost (5) is cheaper than buying a new phone (10).
Buying a new phone (5) is cheaper than repairing (20).

solution.approach:
Read the number of test cases. For each case read X and Y. Compare the two values:
- If X < Y, repairing is cheaper → print REPAIR.
- If X > Y, buying a new phone is cheaper → print NEW PHONE.
- Otherwise they cost the same → print ANY.
The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
